_meta.prompt: Lily math word problem (~45 natural tokens, --ctx=4096 alloc)

cells.runtime: transformers + bnb-4bit NF4 + bf16 compute + SDPA
transformers + bnb-4bit NF4 + bf16 compute + FA2

cells.notes: decode-only timing per ollama's eval_duration; prompt_eval=123 tps; load=13.85s (first call)

blocked_paths.blocker: ValueError: <class 'gptqmodel.nn_modules.qlinear.marlin.MarlinLinear'> does not support platform: win32
JIT build of gptqmodel_exllamav2_ops.pyd fails: missing built extension; needs full MSVC + nvcc + matching torch ABI in a clean shell. Likely tractable but not pursued given ollama win.

verdict: Ollama wins by ~2x over the source-built llama-cpp-python on identical Q4_K_M kernels. The Python wrapper has meaningful overhead. For short-decode workloads on this card, ollama is now the canonical runtime; GPTQ on Windows is dead (Marlin unsupported, ExLlamaV2 needs nontrivial build work). bnb-4bit stays only for cases where the workload must live in HF transformers (e.g. the existing harness paths).
